name: knowledge-event
purpose: Qualify provenance-bound evidence into an appended, unvalidated Knowledge Event (a ΔK candidate) in the Knowledge Ledger.

transformation.statement: Evidence -> qualified Knowledge Event (returned AND appended to the ledger as unvalidated)

consumes.name: evidence
consumes.description: A provenance-bound claim to be qualified. MUST carry at least one source; unsourced evidence is rejected (E_UNSOURCED_EVIDENCE).

claim.description: The asserted knowledge, e.g. 'salt contamination reduces plaster pull-off'.

type.description: What the claim is about (material, mechanism, failure, product, ...).

ref.description: Optional id/handle of the subject in the knowledge graph.

sources.description: Provenance. Each item references an ikl_sources row or an external document. No orphan knowledge (Principle 3).

subjectDomain.description: Whether the evidence originates in Fresco internal knowledge or external sources.

produces.name: event
produces.description: The qualified event, returned to the caller AND appended to the ledger. Always starts unvalidated (validationStatus=pending) — never auto-promoted to validated knowledge (Principle 4).

evidenceRefs.description: The sources that support the event — carried through, never dropped.

deltaK.description: Optional magnitude of the knowledge change.

validationStatus.description: MUST be 'pending' on creation. Promotion is a separate human-gated step.

contested.description: True when evidence conflicts (paradox flagged for team review).

reasoning.evidenceModel: independent sources counted and weighted by source tier (Confidence Engine rubric: internal-validated 0.95 ... hypothesis 0.20)
reasoning.notes: Confidence is provenance-based: derived from how many INDEPENDENT evidences support the claim and each source's tier. This is the exact contrast with ikl-search, whose identical 0.81 would mean mere semantic proximity. NOTE: 'class: causal' is a proxy — the engine's reasoning is really evidential qualification; see finding F1 (contract v0.3 candidate).

confidence.notes: Weighs independent-source count against source tier. Below a qualification threshold the engine does NOT append (E_INSUFFICIENT_SUPPORT).

cost.notes: Evidence qualification + one transactional ledger append. Unmeasured.

latency.notes: Dominated by the atomic append (produce+append must not partially apply). Unmeasured.

dependencies.name: knowledge-ledger
dependencies.notes: Append-only store of Knowledge Events. Does NOT exist yet (gap KG1).

dependencies.name: confidence-rubric (source-tier weights)
dependencies.notes: The Confidence Engine rubric as data — NOT an engine dependency (statelessness).

dependencies.name: IKL knowledge graph (mechanisms/relationships)
dependencies.notes: Optional: link a causal event to existing graph nodes.

failureModes.result: rejected — no orphan knowledge (Principle 3); nothing appended
no append; returns a non-appended low-confidence event
appends event flagged contested=true + warning (paradox for human review)
failed; MUST NOT return an event as if appended (atomic: all-or-nothing)

provenance.notes: The event embeds evidenceRefs; the append is meaningless without them. Provenance is load-bearing here, not decorative.
